authoritative_sources.title: How to Investigate the Copyright Status of a Work
authoritative_sources.source_class: Primary regulators, statutes, agency manuals, or official public guidance tied to the named industry
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Copyright Office is the authoritative U.S. public-record source for copyright status investigations, registration and renewal searches, and limits on what a search can prove.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Copyright public-record and chain-of-title evidence intake
used_for: limits that prevent the lane from making legal ownership determinations

authoritative_sources.title: Recordation Overview
authoritative_sources.source_class: Primary regulators, statutes, agency manuals, or official public guidance tied to the named industry
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Copyright Office defines transfers of copyright ownership, documents pertaining to copyright, notices of termination, effective recordation dates, and the recordation system.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Ownership transfer, license, and termination evidence review
used_for: documents pertaining to ownership, allocation, licensing, or exercise of rights

authoritative_sources.title: Motion Pictures: Registration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Primary regulators, statutes, agency manuals, or official public guidance tied to the named industry
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Copyright Office defines motion pictures and audiovisual works, identifies common works in the category, and anchors registration workflow for film, television, video, and related audiovisual works.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Work classification and registration evidence review
used_for: public baseline for film and television work categories

authoritative_sources.title: Rights, Camera, Action! IP Rights and the Film-Making Process
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: WIPO is the United Nations specialized agency for intellectual property and this film-focused guide explains how producers document and manage rights through development, production, financing, distribution, and exploitation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Film rights workflow and chain-of-title package assembly

authoritative_sources.title: Production Center | Television Live Action: Getting Started
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAG-AFTRA is the official performer union for covered U.S. film and television production and its production-center workflow names performer contracts, Exhibit G reports, final cast lists, and casting data reports.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Performer, cast, and appearance-rights workpaper review
used_for: performer contract and final cast-list evidence
routing when cast clearance, residual, or labor interpretation is required

authoritative_sources.title: U.S. Copyright Office & The MLC
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The MLC was designated by the U.S. Copyright Office under the Music Modernization Act to administer the blanket mechanical license, receive usage reports, distribute mechanical royalties, and maintain a public musical works database.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Music-rights data and mechanical-license boundary review
used_for: refusal language that MLC or cue data does not prove sync or master-use clearance

authoritative_sources.title: Trademark refusal: Title of a single creative work
authoritative_sources.source_class: Primary regulators, statutes, agency manuals, or official public guidance tied to the named industry
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The USPTO is the authoritative U.S. trademark agency and this guidance distinguishes single creative-work titles from series titles and defines evidence expectations that require title or trademark specialist review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Title and trademark boundary evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: Content Availability Metadata: Avails and Title List
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized standards bodies, professional frameworks, or public operating references directly relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: MovieLabs and EMA define the Avails metadata format for transferring availability data from publishers to retailers, including timing, territory, and business-rule metadata that rights completion must support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Rights-window, territory, media, and distribution handoff review
used_for: territory, timing, and business-rule metadata expectations

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 15489-1:2016 Information and documentation - Records management - Part 1: Concepts and principles
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO 15489 is the recognized international records-management standard for governing authentic, reliable, usable, and traceable records, which is directly relevant to rights evidence packages.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Records governance and evidence traceability control

workflow_stages.name: Intake and scope classification
workflow_stages.description: Identify the production, version, asset, platform, jurisdiction, release model, requested use, lifecycle state, approval owner, and whether the request is in-scope, blocked, or adjacent-lane work.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Source-of-truth retrieval and evidence inventory
workflow_stages.description: Retrieve clearance logs, rights bibles, contracts, public-record searches, releases, music workpapers, title reports, avails data, E&O checklists, and system references from tenant systems of record.
source_classes: Primary regulators, statutes, agency manuals, or official public guidance tied to the named industry
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Chain-of-title and copyright-record review
workflow_stages.description: Validate that option, purchase, assignment, registration, recordation, renewal, termination, and underlying-rights evidence is present and traceable without asserting legal ownership.
source_classes: Primary regulators, statutes, agency manuals, or official public guidance tied to the named industry

workflow_stages.name: Use-specific clearance evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Map music, clips, stills, archives, artwork, brands, locations, talent, appearance, life-rights, publicity, title, and trademark items to required evidence by territory, media, term, language, and exclusivity.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Rights-window, avails, and delivery handoff review
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile rights-management and contract data with avails, delivery, platform, broadcaster, streamer, and E&O checklist requirements before preparing downstream handoff records.
source_classes: Recognized standards bodies, professional frameworks, or public operating references directly relevant to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception, rework, refusal, and completion recording
workflow_stages.description: Move cases to rework or blocked state when evidence is missing or conflicted, refuse authority overreach, route adjacent ownership, and produce audit-ready completion records only when evidence supports closure.
source_classes: Primary regulators, statutes, agency manuals, or official public guidance tied to the named industry
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Rights bible, clearance log, chain-of-title tracker, legal delivery checklist, E&O insurer checklist, and exception history
Underlying-rights agreements, option and purchase records, assignments, work-made-for-hire documents, copyright registrations, recordation references, renewal records, and termination notices
Music cue sheets, composer agreements, score ownership records, synchronization licenses, master-use licenses, publisher and label clearances, PRO lookups, MLC notes, and soundtrack restrictions
Clip, still, archive, stock-footage, artwork, brand, trademark, product-placement, location, appearance, name-image-likeness, life-rights, and publicity releases
SAG-AFTRA or other performer workpaper references, final cast lists, performer contracts, Exhibit G references, minor or sensitive-subject restrictions, and talent-use limitations
Title search report, trademark search notes, script clearance report, public-record search notes, copyright catalog or recordation results, and counsel review requests

Avails rows, rights-window extracts, territory and media matrices, language and dubbing rights records, exclusivity or holdback notes, delivery manifests, MAM or DAM asset identifiers, and downstream handoff records
Tenant policy overlays, approval matrices, system-of-record maps, retention schedules, and records-governance escalation notes
systems_of_record: Contract lifecycle management and business-affairs repository
Production legal document repository or deal-room
Media asset management or digital asset management system
Copyright Office public records, registration, and recordation systems
Trademark search system and title-search repository
Union or guild producer portals where performer workpapers are maintained
Avails, distribution, delivery, and platform operations systems
E&O insurer checklist or legal delivery tracker

allowed: Classify clearance requests and identify missing prerequisites.
Maintain and reconcile evidence packages, clearance logs, rights bibles, and completion records.
Prepare evidence-backed recommendations, status updates, exception summaries, documentation completeness summaries, and records-governance escalation notes.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete work based on documented evidence and lifecycle state.
escalate: Missing or conflicting jurisdiction, rights window, territory, media, term, language, exclusivity, ownership, or approval context.
Conflicting public-record results, contract abstracts, rights-management records, avails rows, E&O requirements, or tenant policy.

Requests involving legal interpretation, license negotiation, fair use, title or trademark judgment, talent-rights interpretation, takedown disposition, standards-and-practices, E&O, or financial approval.
refuse: Final clearance approval, chain-of-title certification, fair-use call, trademark opinion, E&O coverage decision, or legal advice.
Marking work complete without evidence tied to every required right, use, owner, system, and downstream requirement.
Overriding missing releases, license restrictions, public-record conflicts, rights-window conflicts, or approval gates because of schedule pressure.

domain_failure_modes: A program is delivered or aired with incomplete chain-of-title, missing option exercise, assignment, registration, recordation, or termination evidence.
Music is treated as cleared because a cue sheet, MLC record, or PRO lookup exists even though sync, master-use, territory, media, or term evidence is absent.
Archive clips, stills, artwork, brand references, product placement, location footage, or talent appearances move downstream without use-specific releases.
Rights-management records, contract abstracts, and avails data disagree on territory, platform, media, language, exclusivity, or holdback restrictions.
Title, trademark, publicity, privacy, defamation, or fair-use questions are answered by a documentation lane instead of being escalated.
A clearance case is marked complete without E&O checklist evidence, public-record search notes, delivery owner signoff, or traceable system-of-record links.
The pack stays generic and does not require real broadcast, film, and streaming artifacts such as cue sheets, chain-of-title files, release inventories, avails rows, and E&O delivery checklists.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
Tenant-specific adaptation preserving the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific publicity, privacy, moral-rights, and fair-use handling
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public sources establish evidence categories and boundaries, but tenant counsel and jurisdiction-specific law determine legal sufficiency. The package refuses definitive legal interpretation.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Distributor and E&O carrier checklist differences
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Delivery and E&O evidence requirements vary by distributor, broadcaster, streamer, policy, insurer, and deal. Runtime retrieval must provide the controlling checklist and approval owner.

Mechanical-rights data, cue sheets, PRO lookups, sync licenses, and master-use licenses cover different rights. The lane records the evidence and routes interpretation or missing approvals to music supervision or legal.

research_conclusion: The source set supports a validated spec pack because it covers public record investigation, copyright registration and recordation, film rights workflow, performer and music workpapers, title/trademark boundary handling, avails handoff metadata, and records-governance controls. Remaining ambiguity is expected tenant or legal context and is handled through explicit refusal, rework, or orchestrator return.
